title: Chapter 2 Quiz: JSX and Core Components
description: Test your mastery of JSX, View, Text, Image, ScrollView, and touchables.

questions.question: Which of the following can be placed directly inside `{}` in JSX?
questions.explanation: Ternary expressions are allowed because they are expressions. `if` and `for` are statements.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Fragment (`<>...</>`) in React Native?
options: To group children without adding an extra View
questions.explanation: Fragments group multiple elements without introducing an additional container view.

questions.question: What is the default value of `flexDirection` in React Native?
questions.explanation: In React Native, the default is `column` (unlike on the web where it's `row`).

questions.question: How do you center a child both horizontally and vertically inside a parent View?
questions.explanation: `justifyContent: 'center'` centers along the main axis, `alignItems: 'center'` centers along the cross axis. With default column direction, this centers both ways.

questions.question: What happens if you put raw text directly inside a `<View>` without a `<Text>` wrapper?
questions.explanation: React Native requires all text to be inside a `<Text>` component; otherwise you'll get an error.

questions.question: How do you include a local image named 'icon.png' from the 'assets' folder?
questions.explanation: For local images, you must use `require()` with the relative path.

questions.question: Which `resizeMode` scales the image uniformly so that both dimensions are at least as large as the view?
questions.explanation: `cover` scales the image to cover the entire view, possibly cropping edges.

questions.question: Why should you avoid using ScrollView for very long lists?
options: It renders all items at once, which can hurt performance
questions.explanation: ScrollView renders all its children upfront, which can be slow for long lists. Use FlatList for lazy loading.

questions.question: When mapping over an array to render components, what prop should you always include?
questions.explanation: The `key` prop helps React identify which items changed for efficient updates.

questions.question: Which touchable component provides a fading effect when pressed?
questions.explanation: TouchableOpacity fades the content, giving a subtle visual feedback.

questions.question: The `<Button>` component can be fully customised with styles.
questions.explanation: Button has limited styling; for custom buttons you should use a Touchable component.

questions.question: Fragments add an extra view to the native hierarchy.
questions.explanation: Fragments do not create any native view; they are purely a grouping mechanism.

questions.question: In React Native, you can use CSS properties like `text-transform` directly.
questions.explanation: React Native supports only a subset of CSS; `text-transform` is not available.

questions.question: The `hitSlop` prop increases the tappable area of a touchable without affecting layout.
questions.explanation: `hitSlop` extends the touchable area outward, making it easier to press.

questions.question: The default value of `flexDirection` in React Native is _____.
questions.explanation: The default is `column`.

questions.question: To make a ScrollView scroll horizontally, set the prop _____ to true.
questions.explanation: The prop is simply `horizontal`.

questions.question: Which component would you use to display a simple platform‑default button with a title?
questions.explanation: The `Button` component renders a native button with a title.

questions.question: What does the `pagingEnabled` prop on ScrollView do?
questions.explanation: `pagingEnabled` makes the scroll view stop at multiples of its own size, like a pager.

questions.question: How can you apply platform‑specific styles in React Native?
questions.explanation: You can use either file extensions (e.g., MyComponent.ios.js) or `Platform.select` to conditionally apply styles.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid style property for the `<Text>` component?
questions.explanation: `textTransform` is not supported; you need to transform the string in JavaScript.
